textPlain: It's that Evan Chen. Thanks for teaching me the way of the bary, senpai!
The author’s doing themselves a disservice by using the word “basic” - it doesn’t describe either the mathematics or the description. Perhaps it refers to its focus on the basics of a field.

> The set ℕ is the set of positive integers, not including 0.Hell yeah!I've agonised over this quite a lot over the decades. Not including 0 is more intuitive, but including 0 is more convenient. Of course, both approaches are correct. My main reason for not including 0 is that I hate seeing sequences numbered starting with 0.

I have been looking for a general all around math text since last century (as an amateur / recreational mathematician). I m starting to look at this. It seems to cover lots of ground. Any observations?

From the books advice corner:"As explained in the preface, the main prerequisite is some amount of mathematical
maturity. This means I expect the reader to know how to read and write a proof, follow logical arguments, and so on."Yeah, that's way beyond what's called basic math instruction, e. g. in schools. A more specific, as in accurate, subtitle (or description) is in order.
Subscription to Math Academy might be more suitable for that.
